other_observers: Maggie Boswell, Lisa Edwards and David Waltman.; First seen by Karen Metz, and first seen and reported by Glen Walbek. Seen by many observers through July 11, when reported by Doug Faulkner.
specific_location: Seen from the Homestead parking lot at Castlewood Canyon State Park.
description: The bird was clearly a vulture, flying with nearby Turkey Vultures. It was quite distant, so the first clue to its identity was the rapid wing flapping with stiff wings, quite unlike the lazy flapping of Turkey Vulture. The bird was all black, except for large white patches at the base of the primaries. The tail was very noticeably shorter than the tails on Turkey Vultures, and the wings were shorter and broader.  The head was dark and not red; the color of the Turkey Vulture heads could be seen at this distance.Behaviors: The bird was seen flapping and soaring in the company of Turkey Vultures as it rose in the sky.Call: None heard.Plumage: Adult
